# hubspot_custom_object_schema (Resource)

Manages a custom HubSpot object schema with continuously owned bootstrap properties.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hubspot_custom_object_schema" "widget" {
  name                         = "widget"
  labels                       = { singular = "Widget", plural = "Widgets" }
  primary_display_property     = "name"
  expected_external_properties = ["category"]

  properties = {
    name = {
      label      = "Name"
      type       = "string"
      field_type = "text"
    }
  }
}

resource "hubspot_property" "widget_category" {
  object_type = hubspot_custom_object_schema.widget.object_type_id
  name        = "category"
  label       = "Category"
  group_name  = "widget_information"
  type        = "string"
  field_type  = "text"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `labels` (Object) Singular and plural display labels. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--labels))
- `name` (String) Immutable schema internal name.
- `primary_display_property` (String) Owned property used as the primary display value.
- `properties` (Attributes Map) Nonempty map of continuously owned bootstrap properties. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--properties))

### Optional

- `allows_sensitive_properties` (Boolean) Allows sensitive properties on eligible Enterprise accounts.
- `associated_objects` (Set of String) Exact object types associated at schema creation.
- `deletion_protection` (Boolean) Blocks schema deletion until disabled in a prior apply.
- `description` (String) Schema description.
- `expected_external_properties` (Set of String) Separately managed properties acknowledged without adoption.
- `required_properties` (Set of String) Owned properties required by the schema.
- `searchable_properties` (Set of String) Owned properties indexed for search.
- `secondary_display_properties` (List of String) Ordered owned properties shown as secondary values.
- `should_create_same_object_association` (Boolean) Requests a same-object association at creation.

### Read-Only

- `fully_qualified_name` (String) HubSpot fully qualified schema name.
- `id` (String) Canonical HubSpot object type ID.
- `object_type_id` (String) HubSpot object type ID returned after creation.

<a id="nestedatt--labels"></a>
### Nested Schema for `labels`

Required:

- `plural` (String)
- `singular` (String)


<a id="nestedatt--properties"></a>
### Nested Schema for `properties`

Required:

- `field_type` (String) HubSpot editor field type.
- `label` (String) Property display label.
- `type` (String) HubSpot property storage type.

Optional:

- `description` (String) Property description.
- `display_order` (Number) HubSpot display order; defaults to -1.
- `form_field` (Boolean) Whether the property can appear in forms.
- `has_unique_value` (Boolean) Whether property values must be unique.
- `hidden` (Boolean) Whether HubSpot hides the property.
- `options` (Map of String) Reserved option metadata for supported property types.
- `show_currency_symbol` (Boolean) Whether HubSpot displays a currency symbol.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Choose one accepted import form.
tofu import hubspot_custom_object_schema.widget '<object-type-id>'

# A fully qualified name is also accepted and is canonicalized in state.
tofu import hubspot_custom_object_schema.widget 'p<hub-id>_widget'
```
